skill.name: gate-exchange-marketanalysis
skill.description: The market analysis function of Gate Exchange — liquidity, momentum, liquidation, funding arbitrage, basis, manipulation risk, order book explainer, slippage simulation. Use when the user asks about liquidity, depth, slippage, buy/sell pressure, liquidation, funding rate arbitrage, basis/premium, manipulation risk, order book explanation, or slippage simulation (e.g. market buy $X slippage). Trigger phrases: liquidity, depth, slippage, momentum, buy/sell pressure, liquidation, squeeze, funding rate, arbitrage, basis, premium, manipulation, order book, spread, slippage simulation.
skill.summary: Comprehensive Gate Exchange market analysis tool covering liquidity, momentum, liquidations, arbitrage, and slippage simulation via read-only MCP tools.
skill.author: gate

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 368 potential issues (Ruby backtick execution, weak crypto, hardcoded URLs), but all are false positives from Markdown code formatting and legitimate references. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code - it provides structured market analysis prompts for Gate MCP tools. All operations are read-only market data analysis with no trading, credential handling, or data exfiltration. Safe to publish.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Blocks Detected as Shell Execution
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected 322 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns. These are all false positives from Markdown code blocks (using backticks `) in CHANGELOG.md, README.md, references/scenarios.md, and SKILL.md. The backticks are used for Markdown code formatting and inline code spans, not shell command execution. No executable Ruby or shell code exists in this skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography and Network Patterns
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected 45 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns and 1 'hardcoded URL'. These are false positives: 'weak crypto' detections are from the word 'hash' in content_hash/tree_hash JSON fields and legitimate Git hash references; 'hardcoded URL' is the official Gate Exchange website (https://www.gate.com) in README.md line 5, a legitimate reference link. No actual cryptographic operations or network requests occur in this skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Patterns
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected 10 'system reconnaissance' patterns in references/scenarios.md. These are false positives from legitimate market analysis terminology like 'spot market', 'futures contract', and trading volume references. No actual system reconnaissance or information gathering occurs.

content.user_title: Analyze Gate Exchange Markets: Liquidity, Momentum, and Slippage
content.value_statement: Get comprehensive Gate Exchange market analysis across ten scenarios including liquidity depth, buy/sell momentum, liquidation monitoring, funding arbitrage opportunities, and slippage simulation. This skill uses read-only MCP tools to provide structured insights without trading capabilities.
actual_capabilities: Analyze order book depth and liquidity with 24h vs 30-day volume comparisons
Calculate buy/sell pressure momentum and funding rate bias for market sentiment
Monitor liquidation patterns and detect potential squeeze scenarios
Screen for funding rate arbitrage opportunities with volume and spread analysis
Simulate market order slippage by walking the order book ladder
Evaluate manipulation risk through depth-to-volume ratios and large order detection
limitations: Requires Gate MCP to be configured and connected before use
All analysis is read-only with no trading execution capabilities
Slippage simulation requires both currency pair and quote amount from user
Market data is limited to what Gate MCP tools provide

use_cases.title: Check Trading Liquidity Before Large Orders
use_cases.description: Before executing a large trade on Gate Exchange, analyze order book depth, 24h volume, and slippage risk to determine if the market can handle your position size without significant price impact.

use_cases.title: Identify Arbitrage Opportunities
use_cases.description: Screen the Gate Exchange market for funding rate arbitrage opportunities by comparing funding rates, spot-futures spreads, and trading volumes to find profitable basis trades.

use_cases.title: Understand Market Manipulation Risk
use_cases.description: Evaluate how susceptible a trading pair is to price manipulation by analyzing depth-to-volume ratios, detecting large orders, and monitoring consecutive same-side trades that could signal artificial price movement.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Liquidity Check
prompt_templates.prompt: How is ETH liquidity on Gate Exchange?
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick liquidity assessment for trading decisions

prompt_templates.title: Momentum Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Is BTC more long or short in the past 24 hours, and is the momentum sustainable?
prompt_templates.scenario: Understanding market sentiment and trading pressure

prompt_templates.title: Slippage Simulation
prompt_templates.prompt: What slippage would I get for a $50,000 market buy of ADA_USDT?
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning trade execution with realistic cost estimates

prompt_templates.title: Funding Arbitrage Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Are there any funding rate arbitrage opportunities on Gate Exchange perpetual contracts right now?
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding profitable basis trades between spot and futures

output_examples.input: How is ETH liquidity on Gate Exchange?
output_examples.output: ETH Liquidity Analysis: Order book depth shows 20 levels with top 10 bid depth totaling $2.3M and ask depth $2.1M. 24h volume is $485M versus 30-day average of $320M (above average). Bid-ask spread is 0.02% with slippage risk at 0.03% (very low). Liquidity rating: 5/5 stars - excellent liquidity suitable for large orders.

output_examples.input: What slippage would I get for a $50,000 market buy of ADA_USDT?
output_examples.output: ADA_USDT Slippage Simulation (Market Buy $50,000): Walking the ask ladder starting at $0.6234. Fill summary: First $10,000 at $0.6234, next $20,000 at $0.6238 (avg $0.6236), final $20,000 at $0.6245 (avg $0.6242). Volume-weighted average price: $0.6238. Slippage vs best ask: 0.0004 USDT (0.064%). Conclusion: Low slippage - market can handle this size efficiently.

output_examples.input: Is BTC showing more buy or sell pressure in the last 24 hours?
output_examples.output: BTC Momentum Analysis (24h): Buy share: 62% vs Sell share: 38% - moderate buy-side pressure. 24h volume: $1.2B vs 30-day avg: $980M (22% above average - active trading). Funding rate: +0.015% (slightly positive, longs pay shorts). Order book top 10 shows bid depth $85M, ask depth $78M. Conclusion: Mildly bullish momentum with sustainable buying pressure, but not overheated.

best_practices: Always specify whether you want spot or futures analysis when asking about perpetual contracts
For slippage simulation, provide both the trading pair and your intended trade size in quote currency
Use the structured outputs from each scenario to make informed decisions rather than relying on single metrics
anti_patterns: Don't assume default trading pairs or amounts - the skill will prompt for required parameters
Don't use this skill for price predictions - it provides current market analysis, not forecasting
Don't ignore liquidity warnings - low depth or high slippage risk indicates the market cannot handle large orders efficiently

faq.question: Does this skill execute trades on Gate Exchange?
faq.answer: No, this skill only performs read-only market analysis using Gate MCP tools. It provides insights and data to help you make trading decisions, but does not execute any trades.

faq.question: What is the difference between spot and futures analysis?
faq.answer: Spot analysis analyzes the immediate market for buying and selling assets, while futures analysis analyzes perpetual or futures contracts. Use keywords like 'perpetual' or 'contract' to trigger futures analysis, or the skill defaults to spot.

faq.question: Why does slippage simulation require both a pair and amount?
faq.answer: Slippage varies significantly between different trading pairs and is highly dependent on order size. Without both parameters, the simulation cannot provide accurate results - the skill will prompt you for missing information.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without Gate MCP configured?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires Gate MCP to be installed and configured to access market data. Use the gate-mcp-installer skill first if you need to set up MCP connection.

faq.question: What does a 5-star liquidity rating mean?
faq.answer: A 5-star rating indicates excellent liquidity with deep order books (20+ levels), high 24h volume above 30-day average, tight bid-ask spreads (under 0.1%), and very low slippage risk (under 0.1%). This means the market can efficiently handle large orders.

faq.question: How often should I run arbitrage scans?
faq.answer: Funding rates and spreads change frequently in crypto markets. For active arbitrage monitoring, run scans every few minutes. Remember that opportunities disappear quickly as other traders act on them, and factor in trading fees when calculating profitability.
